text: SCIP replaces LSIF as Sourcegraph's code intelligence protocol; LSIF is fully removed in Sourcegraph 4.6+
SCIP cross-repo navigation requires a Sourcegraph server instance at query time; SCIP CLI can generate and snapshot indexes standalone but cannot resolve cross-repo jumps without the server
The SCIP scip.proto schema has no file-to-file dependency edge; cross-file relationships are implied indirectly via shared symbol IDs, not explicit edges
Kythe has no documented Windows support and requires Bazel as primary build system (CMake rudimentary only)
Kythe v0.0.75 released March 12, 2026 — project is alive but community adoption outside Google is minimal
aider repomap builds a directed file graph (nodes=files, edges=code references) using tree-sitter + NetworkX PageRank; per-repo, code-only, no queryable graph API
ast-grep has no dependency graph feature as of early 2026; graph query feature request filed March 2026

dependency-cruiser v17.3.10 (March 2026) exports JSON with modules, dependencies, dependents — a machine-consumable bidirectional graph for Node.js/TS projects; MIT license, pure Node.js, Windows-compatible
Sourcetrail original project archived December 14, 2021; community fork adds no API or cross-repo capability
Universal ctags supports JSON Lines output and reference tags on Windows, but produces a symbol index only — no file-to-file graph edges; license is GPL-2.0
SCIP Document.language field accepts any string, meaning non-code files could theoretically be force-encoded, but no tooling exists for this
repominify (Python package) adds code_graph.txt and graph_statistics.yaml export on top of repomix output; not a live queryable graph
Cursor uses server-side embeddings (Turbopuffer) + Merkle tree incremental sync; no file-to-file dependency graph or edges in its index
Cursor index is remote (Turbopuffer), not portable or exportable; no programmatic index access API
Continue stores LanceDB index at ~/.continue/index/lancedb; schema has no edge data — vector + FTS + symbol indexes only, no dependency graph model
Continue has Windows-specific LanceDB native module load failures documented in issue tracker
Aider repomap builds a NetworkX directed graph: nodes=files, edges=cross-file symbol references, ranked by PageRank; session-ephemeral, not exported, not queryable via API or CLI
RepoMapper (MIT) extracts Aider's repomap as standalone CLI + MCP server; no change-impact or cross-project support
GitHub Copilot Workspace technical preview ended May 30, 2025; no graph or dependency model was ever exposed
Zed intentionally avoids automated full-codebase indexing; no graph model
Sourcegraph exposes a GraphQL API for precise cross-repo symbol navigation via SCIP; 'Find References' is effectively a cross-file edge query
Cody Free/Pro discontinued July 23, 2025; only Cody Enterprise remains
Augment Code Context Engine builds a semantic dependency graph across repos; exposed via MCP since February 2026; closed SaaS, no self-hosted or exportable index documented
Greptile REST Genius API ($0.45/req) exposes programmatic graph queries including change-impact across repos; commercial SaaS, per-request pricing makes high-frequency tracking uneconomical
TabbyML roadmap includes expose API to Tabby's index but not shipped as of April 2026
CodeGraphContext (MIT) is an MCP server + CLI that indexes code into KuzuDB/FalkorDB/Neo4j; exposes change-impact queries; requires Python 3.10+
Nx createNodesV2 plugin API can add arbitrary-file-typed nodes to the project graph matching any glob pattern including .md and .yaml; exports via nx graph --file graph.json
Nx cross-workspace (cross-repo) support is enterprise-only via Nx Cloud Polygraph; OSS tier is strictly single-workspace; Synthetic Monorepo feature on 2026 roadmap
Lerna v9 removed bootstrap/add/link; is a thin wrapper over Nx — evaluating Lerna = evaluating Nx OSS tier; no independent graph functionality
Turborepo turbo query GraphQL API exposes package-level and task-level affected detection; individual files cannot be modeled as first-class nodes; no cross-repo support
Pants supports fine-grained per-file dependency inference but is Linux/macOS only — hard blocker for Windows 11 environment
madge programmatic API returns bidirectional adjacency list via .obj() and .depends(path); JS/TS/CSS only; not extensible to YAML or Markdown
Backstage requires WSL on Windows — native Windows installation explicitly unsupported per official getting-started docs
Backstage supports custom entity kinds via CatalogProcessor but requires TypeScript plugin code; UI won't render custom kinds without additional frontend plugin work
Backstage catalog auto-update on file change requires GitHub webhooks; there is no local filesystem watcher for non-SCM-backed files
Port is SaaS-only with no self-hosted option for non-Enterprise customers; free tier (15 seats, 10K entities) runs on Port's cloud
No turnkey minimum-viable catalog open-source tool (single YAML + graph viewer) was found targeting individual developer workflow or skill-file tracking
GitHub dependency graph is strictly package-ecosystem-scoped (npm, pip) and cannot track custom file-to-file or workflow-to-workflow dependencies
GitHub topics work at repo granularity only, not file-level; cannot tag individual .claude/skills/ files as portable across projects
Obsidian v0.9.1 (March 2026) blocked symlinks pointing outside vault boundary; FolderBridge is the current viable replacement path
FolderBridge plugin (March 2026, Windows-hardened) mounts external directories as virtual vault paths with real-time sync; confirmed works with all Obsidian plugins
Breadcrumbs V4 uses full file paths as node identifiers; exposes window.BCAPI for programmatic graph traversal; operates on metadataCache (.md only)
Dataview indexes only .md files for frontmatter/inline fields; DataviewJS can enumerate all files via app.vault.getFiles() but cannot read metadata from non-markdown files
obsidian-skill-graph v0.1.0 (April 1, 2026) scans SKILL.md files for references and injects SKILL.md to referenced-file entries into metadataCache.resolvedLinks
No doc KG tool treats .ts/.sh/.yaml as first-class nodes with metadata indexing; companion stub .md approach is the universal workaround
Logseq DB version is not designed to index existing file trees; writes content into its own database
Tana supertag system is SaaS-only ($10/month); Local API allows programmatic node creation from Node.js scripts; no on-premises option
Dendron entered maintenance mode March 2023; no active feature development; do not invest
skills-md-graph Rust CLI supports --uses (dependents), --deps (transitive), --path-between, cycle detection via Tarjan SCC, DOT/PNG/RDF/Neo4j Cypher export
SoNash codebase has no chokidar dependency, no fs.watch usage in scripts/, and no watch-mode npm script; change detection is entirely on-demand
SoNash session-start.js uses content-hash (SHA) change detection for package-lock.json only; CAS index rebuild is manually triggered with no watch trigger
@parcel/watcher uses ReadDirectoryChangesW on Windows (native OS API); getEventsSince(snapshotPath) enables offline change detection; v2.5.1 Jan 2025, prebuilt win32-x64
@parcel/watcher getEventsSince without Watchman backend = O(n) FindFirstFile traversal; 1000 files < 1s on SSD; 50,000+ files requires Watchman as backend
Chokidar v5 is ESM-only (Node 20+, Nov 2025); v4 supports both ESM+CJS for mixed CJS codebases
Watchman requires persistent system daemon; uses named pipes on Windows; must be running before queries work; high solo-dev operational cost
Official @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem has no file watching capability; 15 stateless tools only
bsmi021/mcp-file-operations-server has start_watching/get_changes tools but has 21 stars and 6 forks; not production-ready
MCP protocol cannot push change events to Claude between turns; practical architecture is external watcher daemon writes to SQLite, Claude polls via MCP
Content-hash is most reliable cross-locale change detection; mtime unreliable on Windows (changes on read, git checkout resets it); SoNash session-start pattern is directly reusable template
Session-start @parcel/watcher snapshot pattern: write snapshot at session-end, call getEventsSince at session-start, feed changed paths to update-index — zero daemon required
No universal cross-domain schema or RDF vocabulary for file portability exists; four independent systems (chezmoi, VSCode, Nx, XDG, Agent Skills) converge on same 4-5 scope values
Nx encodes portability via scope:shared tag; enforced by @nx/enforce-module-boundaries ESLint rule — most formal enforcement mechanism found anywhere
chezmoi portable-by-default: files NOT templates are implicitly universal; .tmpl suffix = machine-specific; inverse heuristic for JASON-OS: absence of project-specific references = portable
VSCode 5-level scope enum: application/machine/machine-overridable/window/resource; machine-scoped settings not synced — clearest operational definition of machine-scoped found
XDG: $XDG_DATA_HOME is explicitly portable across computers per spec; $XDG_STATE_HOME and $XDG_CACHE_HOME are not portable
Agent Skills spec (agentskills.io, Dec 2025, Linux Foundation) has no dedicated portability field; scope encoded through install location (~/.claude/ = user; project/.claude/ = project)
JASON-OS memory file prefixes (feedback_*, user_*, project_*, reference_*) already implicitly encode portability scope — rcm-style tagging without being designed as one
Agent Skills metadata field is an extension point; adding metadata.scope: user or metadata.portability: universal is immediately valid by spec without spec changes

Academic portability literature (2020 systematic review): 'a major obstacle to effective portability measurement is the lack of established mechanisms to specify and measure portability'; no 2023-2026 declarative schema work found
Recommended JASON-OS scope field: 5-value enum universal/user/project/machine/ephemeral; path heuristic has HIGH confidence for auto-classification
~31 of 44 JASON-OS memory files are auto-classifiable by filename prefix with HIGH confidence; ~8-10 reference_* files require human spot-check

@optave/codegraph (npm, March 2026) builds function-level and file-level dependency graph across 34 languages using tree-sitter + SQLite; ships 30+ MCP tools; codegraph registry manages multi-repo; zero network calls, zero telemetry
@optave/codegraph auto-classifies code symbols as entry/core/utility/adapter/dead/leaf based on connectivity; no user-defined tags or file-level metadata tags
@optave/codegraph codegraph watch enables sub-second incremental rebuilds on file change
codebase-memory-mcp is a pure C static binary with background file watcher and multi-project awareness via list_projects; no user-defined tags; no Node.js integration
KuzuDB was abandoned by its maintainers in October 2025; community fork Ladybug exists but is immature; CodeGraphContext defaults to KuzuDB on Windows — reliability risk
No tool found that tracks file identity/portability across projects — the specific use case of tracking a skill file copied from Project A to Project B is absent from every tool examined
User-defined tags on arbitrary files (non-code YAML, Markdown skills, JSON schemas) in a queryable graph: no tool supports this
Quivr RAG framework has tagging and folder organization but requires PostgreSQL + pgvector + Docker stack; not viable for solo Windows dev
PrivateGPT has --watch flag for folder ingestion but is Python/CUDA stack; no tagging, no cross-project, not a dependency graph
dep-tree (gabotechs) fails three of five composite requirements: no tags, no watch, no cross-project; single-project visualization only with no JSON API
No composite hybrid system fully satisfies all five requirements simultaneously; custom thin extension layer on @optave/codegraph is the recommended architecture
LocalNest (wmt-mobile) is a Dart-compiled MCP server with 74 tools, temporal knowledge graph, and cross-project awareness; tag support and watch mode undocumented
SCIP format is open source (Apache 2.0) and used by both Sourcegraph and GitLab — de facto industry standard for code intelligence exchange format
Bazel query exposes rdeps() for reverse dependency queries; supports filegroup for non-code files but requires explicit BUILD declarations for every tracked file
Turborepo turbo query --affected output includes typed change reasons (FileChanged, DependencyChanged) in JSON; cleanest GraphQL API design of any build tool evaluated
PocketBase (Go binary, SQLite, REST API, real-time subscriptions) is a viable substrate for a custom catalog without Docker or Node.js server
Roadie Local is a Docker-image distribution of Backstage, free for <15 users; reduces operational burden but still requires Docker and WSL on Windows
Backstage catalog-info.yaml format (apiVersion, kind, metadata.tags, spec.dependsOn) is a well-designed YAML convention borrowable without running the full Backstage stack
Tana Local API exposes MCP endpoint from desktop app; Claude Code agent could write to Tana programmatically while human uses Tana GUI — hybrid local-computation + SaaS-GUI pattern
Foam has a known bug: clicking non-markdown file links creates a new note instead of opening the file; graph is visual-only with no query API; untyped bidirectional links only
skills-md-graph Neo4j Cypher export enables escalation from flat-file to graph DB without rebuilding the indexing layer
Per-project @parcel/watcher daemon + shared SQLite with WAL mode is a viable cross-project registry watching architecture; race risk mitigated by WAL mode
git post-commit hook is reliable for committed changes but misses untracked files; cross-project requires hook installed in each project
@parcel/watcher-wasm exists for environments without native binaries; relevant if JASON-OS ever runs in sandboxed or containerized context
rcm dotfiles: tag-X directory prefix marks files as belonging to scope X; host-HOSTNAME/ = machine-specific; root = universal — 3-level scope hierarchy
12-factor app behavioral test: a file containing hardcoded machine/environment-specific values is not portable until that value is externalized to env vars
AGENTS.md is a universal cross-tool standard (recognized by Codex CLI, Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Claude Code); CLAUDE.md is Claude-specific — Claude-specific syntax = user-scoped

CocoIndex incremental processing model (tracks which document chunks changed and propagates updates without re-processing full corpus) is most sophisticated self-update model found; requires Python + PostgreSQL + graph DB
Beads (@beads/bd) is a Dolt-backed distributed graph task tracker for AI agents; wrong category for file registry — tracks task relations, not file dependencies
xgmem is a TypeScript MCP server for cross-project agent memory (entity-relation model); not a file registry or dependency graph
Onyx (formerly Danswer) is enterprise search with hybrid search over uploaded docs; Docker-required multi-service stack; no file dependency graph
Nix Flakes solve environment portability (reproducible derivations) not file/skill portability; WSL2 only on Windows
dependency-cruiser --include-only + JSON API: ICruiseResult.modules[].dependents[] is bidirectional — both upstream and downstream edges available without custom edge construction
Obsidian Dataview queries auto-update in real-time as vault changes; file.inlinks and file.outlinks enable upstream/downstream traversal over wikilinks
Obsidian Bases (GA, previously beta) is markdown-only for metadata — not additive over Dataview for non-markdown file tracking
No community MCP watcher server with meaningful adoption found; cyanheads/filesystem-mcp-server identified but not fully evaluated
Nx Synthetic Monorepo roadmap language (each repo brings its knowledge, together they coordinate) is philosophically aligned with JASON-OS portability vision but currently enterprise-only
madge .depends(path) returns all modules that import a given module — directly answers change-in-X-propagates-to-what for JS/TS code graph
Port announced software catalog dependency graph feature with visual upstream/downstream display; only tool in D4 survey that shipped first-class dependency graph as product feature; SaaS only
Buck2 BXL scripting allows custom Starlark to walk build graph programmatically; no stable release tag as of early 2026; rough edges outside Meta
Sourcegraph SCIP supports incremental indexing (only re-index changed files); Find References API can answer what is affected by a changed symbol but no automated push notification
Continue incremental indexing detects changed files via tag_catalog comparison for re-indexing; no change-impact propagation feature
Aider repomap cache stored at {repo_root}/.aider.tags.cache.v{version}/ using diskcache keyed on file modification time
dependency-cruiser cross-project orchestration (merging ICruiseResult JSON across multiple repos into unified graph) is not documented — key gap if adopting as JASON-OS extraction layer
Cortex is closed-source SaaS with no free tier and no self-hosted option; ~$25/developer/month enterprise pricing; immediately disqualified
